title: Alectorolophus ellipticus
intro: Alectorolophus ellipticus, also known as Wild Yam or Dioscorea, is a perennial vine native to the Americas, traditionally classified as a plant species but more accurately described as a type of Dioscorea tuberous species. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including menstrual cramps, menopausal symptoms, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and promoting hormonal balance. The bioactive constituents of Alectorolophus ellipticus include diosgenin, saponins, and other steroidal compounds, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Alectorolophus ellipticus is commonly prepared into teas, tinctures, and capsules, as well as being used in topical creams and ointments for skin issues and pain relief.
